topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam stared at his phone screen, the glow illuminating the deepening lines of exhaustion around his eyes. He had spent the better part of the afternoon trying to locate a misplaced syllabus, only to stumble upon a notification that made his jaw tighten. It was a post from Ms. Albright, his notoriously exacting history teacher, tagging him in a picture taken in the school library. A small, almost casual inclusion. Liam felt a hot flush creep up his neck. He slammed his textbook shut, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet study hall. He scrolled back through her recent posts, a knot of irritation tightening in his chest. Why was she sharing these things? It felt invasive, a violation of the professional boundary he desperately tried to maintain. He quickly minimized the app, his thumb hovering over the 'delete' button, a restless energy buzzing beneath his skin.

[story 2]

"Seriously?" Maya muttered, her voice tight as she stared at the screen. The notification was unmistakable: Professor Davies had liked her recent comment on a class discussion board, and now there was a story shared from his personal account featuring a blurry photo of the lecture hall. Maya felt a sudden, sharp clenching in her stomach. She leaned back in her chair, the cheap plastic groaning under her weight. Every muscle in her jaw felt tense. She picked up her pen, intending to start an essay, but the words refused to form. She kept glancing at the phone, a low, simmering annoyance building behind her eyes. It wasn't just the attention; it was the sheer, unwarranted visibility of it. She shoved the device into her bag with unnecessary force, the strap digging into her shoulder.

[story 3]

"I can't believe this," whispered Ethan, his voice laced with disbelief as he reread the profile picture. It was him—the stern, impeccably dressed professor—smiling slightly in the background of a vacation snapshot. Ethan felt a wave of intense vexation wash over him. He tapped furiously at his screen, trying to find an explanation, a justification for this intrusion. The air in the coffee shop suddenly felt too thick, the ambient noise grating on his nerves. He leaned forward, his knuckles whitening as he gripped the edge of the table. He let out a slow, controlled breath, trying to steady the rapid pulse beating in his neck. This felt like an unnecessary complication, an unwelcome spotlight he wasn't invited to share.
